knockouts.rationale: Prevents conversion of protoheme (heme B) to heme O, blocking the major product-consuming pathway that diverts heme toward cytochrome c oxidase assembly
Prevents diversion of uroporphyrinogen III toward siroheme biosynthesis, preserving flux through the heme branch

overexpressions.rationale: Rate-limiting first committed step in heme biosynthesis; feedback-inhibited by hemin (Km_glycine=3.0 mM, Km_succinyl-CoA=2.0 uM); overexpression increases ALA supply into the pathway
Terminal enzyme inserting Fe2+ into protoporphyrin IX; overexpression ensures protoporphyrin is efficiently converted to heme rather than accumulating as a toxic intermediate

protein_engineering_notes.hem1_feedback_inhibition: HEM1 (P09950) is inhibited by hemin (heme). Engineering a feedback-resistant variant is critical for high-titer heme production. PDB structures available (5TXR at 1.9A resolution) enable rational design of de-inhibited mutants.
protein_engineering_notes.hem1_cofactor: HEM1 requires pyridoxal 5'-phosphate (PLP) as cofactor. PLP supply is generally not limiting in S. cerevisiae.
protein_engineering_notes.hem2_stability: HEM2 (P05373) has instability index 51.5 (possibly unstable); homooctamer requires Zn2+ cofactor. Zinc supplementation may benefit overexpression.
protein_engineering_notes.hem12_rsp5_regulation: HEM12 (P32347) is regulated by RSP5 ubiquitin ligase via its PY motif (residues 36-37). LP36-37AA mutation destroys this motif, potentially stabilizing the protein but causing growth defects.
protein_engineering_notes.hem13_instability: HEM13 (P11353) has highest instability index (54.9) among pathway enzymes; codon optimization or fusion protein strategies may improve stability during overexpression.
protein_engineering_notes.hem14_fad_cofactor: HEM14 (P40012) requires FAD cofactor. Adequate FAD supply should be verified when overexpressing this enzyme.
protein_engineering_notes.hem15_frataxin_interaction: HEM15 (P16622) interacts with YFH1/frataxin (STRING score 0.992), the mitochondrial iron chaperone that delivers Fe2+ for heme synthesis. Co-overexpression of YFH1 may improve iron delivery to ferrochelatase.
protein_engineering_notes.hem25_glycine_transport: HEM25 (Q07534) is the mitochondrial glycine transporter (Km=0.75 mM glycine, Vmax=169.8 nmol/min/mg). Overexpression may relieve glycine supply limitation in the mitochondrial matrix where HEM1 operates.
protein_engineering_notes.all_enzymes_soluble: All 8 heme pathway enzymes lack transmembrane domains and are soluble proteins, simplifying overexpression strategy.

optimization_rounds.strategy: Pathway only (exchange reaction added, no modifications)
KO COX10 (YPL172C) + KO MET1 (YKR069W) + OE HEM1 (min_flux=0.5) + OE HEM15 (min_flux=0.5)
optimization_rounds.notes: 6.0% improvement over baseline; near theoretical maximum

optimization_rounds.strategy: Round 1 + KO GCV1 (YDR019C) + OE HEM12 (min_flux=0.5) + OE HEM3 (min_flux=0.5)
optimization_rounds.notes: No improvement over Round 1; GCV1 KO and additional OE had no effect at this flux level

optimization_rounds.strategy: Round 1 + KO GCV1 + OE HEM13 (min_flux=0.5) + OE HEM14 (min_flux=0.25)
optimization_rounds.notes: No further improvement; stopped per stopping criteria (product flux >=95% of theoretical max)

fba_results.best_strategy: Round 1: KO COX10 + KO MET1 + OE HEM1 + OE HEM15
fba_results.strategy_description: Heme (protoheme/heme B) production in S. cerevisiae using the entirely native heme biosynthesis pathway (KEGG module M00868, 8 steps from glycine + succinyl-CoA). All enzymatic steps (HEM1-HEM4, HEM12-HEM15) are present in iMM904. Strategy: (1) KO COX10/YPL172C to block heme O synthase (product degradation); (2) KO MET1/YKR069W to block uroporphyrinogen III diversion to siroheme; (3) OE HEM1/YDR232W (rate-limiting ALA synthase, min flux 0.5); (4) OE HEM15/YOR176W (ferrochelatase, min flux 0.5). Theoretical max 0.5502, optimized 0.5452 at 10% biomass (99.1% of theoretical max, 6.0% improvement over baseline 0.5141). Key protein-level finding: HEM1 is feedback-inhibited by hemin, making de-regulation critical. HEM15 interacts with YFH1/frataxin for iron delivery. Aerobic conditions required (4 O2 per heme). Iron supplementation recommended.
